title: Presigned Downloads, CloudFront Signatures, Cookies, and Revocation · Runtime Contract: Canned Custom Policy
summary: A product places long-lived S3 presigned URLs in logs, uses one shared CloudFront key, signs with legacy assumptions, and promises immediate revocation after links escape. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the canned custom policy increment in time-bounded signed-delivery decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.
Choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Canned Custom Policy · Theory Canned Custom Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 7291 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound signed access by time, resource, key group, and explicit SHA-256 review; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.
